findings.title: Rewindable body check does not account for `prepared_request.body` being `None` after non-307/308 redirect clears it
findings.detail: After a non-307/308 redirect, `prepared_request.body` is set to `None` (line 148) and the Content-Length/Transfer-Encoding headers are purged (lines 145-147). The `rewindable` check relies on those headers being absent to skip rewinding. This indirect coupling works today, but if a future change (or an auth hook that re-adds a Content-Length) triggers the rewindable check while body is `None`, `rewind_body` will be called with a `None` body. Inside `rewind_body`, `getattr(None, 'seek', None)` returns `None`, and the `else` branch raises `UnrewindableBodyError`. Adding an explicit `and prepared_request.body is not None` guard to the `rewindable` check would make the intent clear and prevent surprise errors.

findings.title: Non-307/308 redirect with a seekable body: `_body_position` is non-None but body is cleared; `rewindable` is False because headers are purged — correct, but fragile and undocumented
findings.detail: For a non-temporary redirect (e.g. 302), `prepared_request.body` is set to `None` at line 148 and the Content-Length/Transfer-Encoding headers are removed. The `rewindable` check then correctly becomes `False` because neither header is present, so `rewind_body` is not called. This works, but the correctness depends on the header-purge happening before the rewindable check and the two conditions staying in sync. A comment explaining this invariant would help future maintainers avoid breaking it accidentally.

findings.title: Rewindable check uses stale `headers` variable after body may have been cleared
findings.detail: At line 150 `headers = prepared_request.headers` is captured, then at lines 145-148 the body is set to `None` and the Content-Length/Transfer-Encoding headers are removed for non-307/308 redirects. The `rewindable` check at lines 170-173 still checks those (now already-removed) header keys in the same `headers` reference, but since it is a reference to the same dict it correctly sees the post-purge state. However, **for non-307/308 redirects** the body was already set to `None` (line 148), yet `_body_position` is still non-None, so `rewindable` can evaluate to `False` (headers purged) but the logic still won't rewind—that part is arguably fine. The real problem is the **opposite** scenario: for 307/308 redirects the body is NOT cleared, headers are NOT purged, so `rewindable` is `True` and `rewind_body` is correctly called. But if `rebuild_auth` (line 165) or any hook modifies `prepared_request.body` between the header-purge block and the rewind call, the rewind applies to potentially the wrong object. This is a minor ordering concern but the sequence is correct as written; low-confidence issue.

findings.title: Missing blank line before function definition (PEP 8)
findings.detail: There is no blank line between `urldefragauth` and `rewind_body`. PEP 8 requires two blank lines between top-level definitions. The rest of the file follows this convention.

findings.title: Assertion on exception message checks `str(e)` of `ExceptionInfo`, not the exception message
findings.detail: In `test_rewind_body_no_seek` (line 1458) and `test_rewind_body_failed_tell` (line 1503), the assertion is `assert '...' in str(e)` where `e` is a `pytest.ExceptionInfo` object. `str(ExceptionInfo)` includes the full representation including the type name, so the string match may pass even if the message wording changes. The idiomatic pytest form is `assert '...' in str(e.value)` to check the actual exception message. The same applies to `test_rewind_body_failed_seek` at line 1482.

findings.title: No test for 307 redirect with a body that has `tell` but no `seek` (the hanging scenario)
findings.detail: The PR description mentions that a body with `tell` but no `seek` can still cause a hang during redirect. There is a unit test for `rewind_body` with a no-seek object, but no integration-level test that actually exercises the `resolve_redirects` path with such a body, verifying that `UnrewindableBodyError` is raised rather than a hang.

findings.title: Callable `tell` attribute check uses `is not None` instead of `callable()`
findings.detail: `getattr(body, 'tell', None) is not None` would also be True if `tell` is a non-callable attribute (e.g. a plain integer or string named `tell`). A more robust check would be `callable(getattr(body, 'tell', None))`. While rare in practice, this is inconsistent with the `seek` check in `rewind_body` (`getattr(..., 'seek', None)`) which then calls it directly, so both should use `callable()`.

findings.title: Typo: 'occured' should be 'occurred'
findings.detail: In the `UnrewindableBodyError` message: `"An error occured when rewinding request body for redirect."` — 'occured' should be 'occurred'. The same typo is reflected in the test assertion at test_requests.py line 1482.

findings.title: Unused variable `s` (Session) in unit tests that don't need a session
findings.detail: In `test_prepare_body_position_non_stream`, `test_rewind_body`, `test_rewind_partially_read_body`, `test_rewind_body_no_seek`, `test_rewind_body_failed_seek`, and `test_rewind_body_failed_tell`, a `requests.Session()` is created and assigned to `s` but never used. The `PreparedRequest` is created directly via `requests.Request(...).prepare()`. The dead assignment should be removed.

findings.title: Missing blank line before `UnrewindableBodyError` class (PEP 8)
findings.detail: `RetryError` and `UnrewindableBodyError` are separated by only one blank line, while the rest of the exception classes in the file are separated by two blank lines.
